caveat: All figures are contested, lag events on the ground, and mix belligerent claims with independent estimates. Treat every number as an order of magnitude, not a fact.
background: The 2026 Iran war began on 28 February when the United States and Israel launched Operation Epic Fury — coordinated strikes that killed Supreme Leader Ali Khamenei in the opening hours. Iran retaliated against Israel, US bases, and Gulf states; Hezbollah opened a Lebanon front days later. A memorandum signed 17 June at Versailles and Tehran collapsed on 8 July, and fighting has since re-centered on control of the Strait of Hormuz, which the IRGC declared closed on 12 July.

status.headline: War widening — Iran hits Gulf states, US weighs expansion
status.detail: The June memorandum broke down on 8 July. The US air campaign has expanded from military targets to Iranian infrastructure — bridges, an airport and the power grid around Bandar Abbas — while Marines board tankers to enforce the reimposed blockade. Iran, which keeps the Strait of Hormuz closed, has widened its retaliation region-wide: missiles and drones at US Gulf targets, a strike on a Kuwait desalination plant, and attacks reported in Syria and Bahrain, with the IRGC threatening 'more crushing' blows on neighbors hosting US forces. The Wall Street Journal reports Trump is leaning toward expanding operations; a US detainee release announced 15 July remains disputed.

stats.label: Killed in Iran (Iranian figures)
stats.note: US/Israeli estimates put Iranian military deaths alone above 6,000

stats.label: Iranian naval vessels destroyed or damaged
Global oil supply disruption
stats.note: Hormuz carries roughly a fifth of global oil flows

stats.label: Strait of Hormuz status
stats.note: Declared closed by the IRGC on 12 July; US enforcing counter-blockade

stats.label: IAEA safeguards access
stats.note: Iran declared normal safeguards 'legally untenable' after strikes on Natanz and other sites

stats.label: Bushehr nuclear power plant
stats.note: Satellite images show fresh damage from strikes dated 7–12 July; Bushehr is Iran's largest nuclear plant

actors.members: United States, Israel; Saudi Arabia, UAE, Kuwait and Bahrain after Iranian strikes; support from UK, Jordan, Qatar, Turkey and others
Iran (Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei, President Pezeshkian), Hezbollah, Houthis, Popular Mobilization Forces, Hamas, Palestinian Islamic Jihad
